name: Braised Pork and Fresh Oysters in Clay Pot
recipeIngredient: 1/2 lb Boneless pork butt, cut into 1 1/2-inch cubes
8 Medium fresh Pacific oysters (OR 1 10 oz. jar)
Chinese or Italian parsley, for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the pork by cutting it into 1 1/2-inch cubes.
2. Clean and open the oysters (or open the jar of pre-cleaned oysters).
3. In a large bowl, mix warm water, brown bean sauce, dark soy sauce, minced ginger, dried orange peel, and medium sherry.
4. Add cornstarch paste to the mixture and stir until well combined.
5. Place the pork cubes into the bowl with the marinade, ensuring each piece is coated.
6. Pour peanut oil into a clay pot over low heat.
7. Gently place the marinated pork pieces into the hot oil in the clay pot.
8. Cook the pork slowly until browned on all sides.
9. Add the oysters to the clay pot and stir gently to coat with the remaining sauce.
10. Continue cooking on low heat for a few more minutes, allowing flavors to meld.
11. Garnish with Chinese or Italian parsley before serving.
Note: Ensure the heat is turned off when finished cooking.
